name: the-advocates-justice
title: The Advocate's Justice: Legal Suspense Murder Mystery
author: Teresa Burrell
description: Attorney Sabre Brown defends fifteen-year-old Conner, accused of murdering his grandmother's abusive boyfriend. With all evidence pointing to the boy and JP's family secrets threatening to unravel, Sabre must untangle a web of lies to uncover the truth.
keyPrinciples: The Advocate's Role — The lawyer is both legal representative and truth-seeker
Evidence vs. Truth — What the evidence shows may not be the whole story
Family Ties — Blood relations can be both a source of strength and a web of deceit
The Vulnerable Client — Defending those who society has already judged
Buried Secrets — The past always catches up; old grudges shape present crimes
Personal Cost — The pursuit of justice exacts a toll on the advocate
The Courtroom as Arena — Legal procedure and strategy drive the narrative
